age_range_hint: ages 7-18 (Cowie & Myers 2016; Year 2 through Year 12; sparse 4-checkpoint structure — Years 3-5, 7-8, 10-11 not explicitly covered)
detection_rationale: Source slug 'strand-year-9' / reference 'cowie-myers-2016-circle-solutions-student-wellbeing' matches Circle Solutions SEL pattern (Cowie & Myers, 2016). 4-checkpoint structure: Year 2, 6, 9, 12.

band_self_reflection_prompts.Year 2: Tell me five things you like about yourself. What is one thing that is really important to you?
band_self_reflection_prompts.Year 6: Draw three pictures of yourself in different situations. How do you behave differently in each, and why?
band_self_reflection_prompts.Year 9: Reflect on your developing identity. Who is a role model for you and what strengths are you working on, and why?
band_self_reflection_prompts.Year 12: Write about who you are now and the person you hope to become. What values will guide your decisions in the future?

band_details.label: Year 2
band_details.developmental_descriptor: Learners at Year 2 develop foundational emotional vocabulary, basic self-regulation strategies, and an awareness of fairness and belonging. Observable indicators focus on concrete personal and relational skills with adult scaffolding.

band_details.label: Year 6
band_details.developmental_descriptor: Learners at Year 6 demonstrate expanded emotional knowledge, perspective-taking, and collaborative skills. They apply understanding of empathy, conflict, and positive communication across a wider range of social contexts.

band_details.label: Year 9
band_details.developmental_descriptor: Learners at Year 9 engage with complex emotional and social challenges, including assertiveness, identity, and the neuroscience of emotion. They demonstrate self-regulation, ethical reasoning, and leadership skills with increasing independence.

band_details.label: Year 12
band_details.developmental_descriptor: Learners at Year 12 demonstrate advanced self-awareness, sustained resilience strategies, and a developed philosophy for life. They apply ethical reasoning, collaborative leadership, and meaningful reflection to personal and community decisions.

progression_philosophy: The Circle Solutions SEL Framework (Cowie & Myers, 2016) uses four year-level markers — Year 2, Year 6, Year 9, Year 12 — as assessment checkpoints across twelve dimensions of social-emotional learning. The framework does not prescribe a continuous annual progression; the four checkpoints are developmental waypoints with observable indicator tasks at each level. Bands between checkpoints (Years 3-5, 7-8, 10-11) are not explicitly described — this is a sparse source structure and interpolation requires teacher judgement. Per Cowie, H. & Myers, C-A. (2016) Circle Solutions for Student Wellbeing (Table 2.2).
